pi_items.source: setup_task.sh which generates data using a python script
setup_task.sh which loops over a list of exactly 12 vessels
setup_task.sh python script has a loop for i in range(150)
setup_task.sh uses 3.114 to calculate fuel based on EEOI, and task description explicitly asks to use 3.114
Running the setup_task.sh Python data generation script yields a total fuel usage of 224,154.9 MT
Running the setup_task.sh Python data generation script yields a total CO2 emission of 698,018.36 tonnes (224154.9 * 3.114)
Running the setup_task.sh Python data generation script yields EEOI values from 7.59 to 17.10, which fits within the expected range.

privileged_info_summary: The task successfully generates 150 synthetic voyages across a fleet of 12 vessels using a CO2 conversion factor of 3.114. However, the anticipated fuel and CO2 ranges are drastically wrong. According to the synthetic data generation logic, the total fuel consumed across all voyages is exactly 224,154.9 MT, yielding total CO2 emissions of 698,018.36 tonnes. This severely contradicts the metadata expectations of [35,000, 48,000] and [100,000, 160,000]. Consequently, the task is defective because the verifier.py strictly expects the total CO2 calculation to fall within [100,000, 160,000], meaning an accurate calculation will automatically fail check #5.
